problem.summary: Cover Top-K selection across a wide range of k values, from argmax and tiny-k decode paths through medium-k reranking and large-k/full-sort boundaries. This task separates wide-k selection from the existing k<=8 fused sampling seed.
inputs: float or half logits/score matrix shaped [rows, vocab_or_segment]
int k, including k=1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, and k close to n
optional masks or ragged segment offsets
outputs: optional selected-count or threshold metadata for partition/radix paths
constraints: Template-only; no timing or correctness evidence is claimed until a CUDA harness and result artifacts are attached
Do not reuse the block-topk-sampling k<=8 fused-sampling result as a wide-k claim
Record sortedness, tie policy, NaN policy, mask policy, k/n ratio, output materialization, temporary storage, and allocation timing boundary

Compare against hipCUB DeviceTopK for unsorted membership, hipCUB DeviceRadixSort or rocThrust sort for sorted/full-sort contracts, PyTorch torch.topk for framework baseline, and FlashInfer/vLLM/vLLM on ROCm where the operation is sampling-facing
For k above 32, justify why the candidate is a network/list/heap, two-pass block candidate, radix-select, threshold-partition, or full-sort path

verification.method: Use a CPU stable sorted reference and at least one library oracle. Check exact values/indices for deterministic contracts, top-k set membership for unsorted contracts, and boundary ties around the kth element.

curation.notes: ROCm parity port from CUDA task `wide-k-topk-selection`. This is a template/harness scaffold only; CUDA-origin result artifacts were intentionally not copied, and no ROCm timing, correctness, or profiler-counter evidence is claimed yet. Run on AMD hardware, attach ROCm version, hipcc flags, gfx target, and label HIP-event-only results as timing-only.
